revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23984597483|manual triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Ezuriko Kofun Cluster]][[ja:江釣子古墳群]]
{{Short description| part_of of Kofuns in northern Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Ezuriko Kofun group | native_name = 江釣子古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image =Eduriko Kohun Park.jpg; File:江釣子古墳群／北上市 - panoramio.jpg| image_size = | alt = | caption = Ezuriko Kofun Park | map_type = Japan Iwate Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ezuriko Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = 70 | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|39|17|29|N|141|04|56|E||display=inline}}; {{Coord|39.291417|N|141.082361|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location ={{ill|Kitakami, Iwate|en|Kitakami, Iwate|qid=Q695917|lt=Kitakami, Iwate|1=Kitakami, Iwate}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}; {{ill|Kitakami|qid=Q695917|1=Kitakami, Iwate}}| region = {{ill|Tōhoku region|en|Tōhoku region|qid=Q129465|lt=Tōhoku region|1=Tōhoku region}} | type = [[kofun]] group | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 7th to 8th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations =1954, 1965 | archaeologists ={{ill|Waseda University|en|Waseda University|qid=Q274486|lt=Waseda University|1=Waseda University}}, [[:en:Tōhoku University|Tōhoku University]] | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (Museum and park) | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|qid=Q30834580|1=Historic Site of Japan}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Ezuriko Kofun group'''|江釣子古墳群|Ezuriko kofun-gun}} is a group of four separate sets of late [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofuns]] in the city of {{ill|Kitakami, Iwate|en|Kitakami, Iwate|qid=Q695917|lt=Kitakami, Iwate|1=Kitakami, Iwate}}, in the {{ill|Tōhoku region|en|Tōhoku region|qid=Q129465|lt=Tōhoku region|1=Tōhoku region}} of northern Japan. The tombs have been collectively designated a [[:en:Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1979, with the area under designation expanded in 1980. <ref>{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/137837|title=江釣子古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref> ==Overview== Located on a {{ill|Fluvial terrace|en|Fluvial terrace|qid=Q1069618|lt=river terrace|1=Fluvial terrace}} near the confluence of the {{ill|Waga River|en|Waga River|qid=Q7959460|lt=Waga River|1=Waga River}} with the {{ill|Kitakami River|en|Kitakami River|qid=Q1348297|lt=Kitakami River|1=Kitakami River}} at an altitude of 70 to 90 meters, the site consists of the Nekoyazu and Naganuma sites (29 tombs), Gojōmaru site (81 tombs), and Yahata site (23 tombs), with a total of some 120 Kofuns. All of ''kofun'' are dome-typed, with a diameter of 4.5 to 16 meters and an average height of 0.4 to 0.7 meters. [[Archaeological excavation]]s were conducted in 1954 by {{ill|Waseda University|en|Waseda University|qid=Q274486|lt=Waseda University|1=Waseda University}} on a portion of the site, and identified the ''kofun'' as being from the late 7th through early 8th centuries. Further investigation was performed by [[:en:Tōhoku University|Tōhoku University]] in 1965. The ''kofun'' contained stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s made from piled rounded river stones, the largest of which was five meters in length, with a central trench to hold the {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|qid=Q48634|lt=Sarcophagus|1=Sarcophagus}}. In the Gojōmaru district, one tomb was found to contain four chambers. {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|qid=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} included remnants of bladed instruments (swords, daggers, agricultural implements), jewelry (''[[magatama]]'' beads, cylindrical beads, glass beads, etc.), horse fittings, and shards of {{ill|Earthenware|en|Earthenware|qid=Q1377111|lt=Earthenware|1=Earthenware}} pottery. An excavation in 1972 confirmed that the ''kofun'' in the Naganuma site were part of the same cluster, and the National Historic Site designation was modified in 1980 to include this group. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> The site is located a three-minute walk from the "Gojo Maru" bus stop on Iwateken Kotsu Bus from {{ill|Kitakami Station|en|Kitakami Station|qid=Q1136531|lt=Kitakami Station|1=Kitakami Station}} on the [[:en:Tohoku Shinkansen|Tohoku Shinkansen]].<ref name="Shiseki"/> ==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan}} *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Iwate)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Iwate)|qid=Q6573266|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Iwate)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Iwate)}} ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== [[Category:}} *[http://www.city.kitakami.iwate.jp/docs/2014053001202/ Kitakami city home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.kitakami-kanko.jp/kanko.php?itemid=200 Kitakami tourism home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.bunka.pref.iwate.jp/archive/hist107 Iwate Cultural Directory] {{in lang|ja}}
{{Kofun navbar}}
[[en:Ezuriko Kofun Cluster]] <!--enwiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Ezuriko Kofun Cluster}} [[ja:江釣子古墳群]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q11551374}} [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:History of Iwate Prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Tourist attractions in Iwate Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
